_comment: Human verdicts on ttn_audit candidate pairs that are NOT merges — semantic false positives the tool cannot detect (a shared catalogue ref / performer across genuinely distinct works). Recorded by candidate_id, the stable per-pair hash. ttn_audit is currently stateless and does not yet read this file; it is the seed for the planned decisions-file filter (see docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-16-ttn-audit-design.md, 'Candidate identity'). A multi-member group lists every contributing pair id. Append a new entry whenever a candidate is triaged and rejected.

rejected.reason: the four distinct movements of The Four Seasons rearranged — one shared title stem, no machine signal; lengths 12-14m differ
titles: The Four Seasons rearranged - Autumn
The Four Seasons rearranged - Spring
The Four Seasons rearranged - Summer
The Four Seasons rearranged - Winter

rejected.reason: two distinct arias from Rinaldo (HWV 7); shared opera + performer, different pieces; lengths 10m vs 4m
titles: Cara sposa, amante cara, from 'Rinaldo, HWV 7'
Venti, turbini, prestate, from 'Rinaldo, HWV 7'

rejected.reason: two distinct arias from Serse (HWV 40); shared opera + performer, different pieces; lengths 4m vs 6m
titles: Crude furie degli orridi abissi from Serse (HWV.40)
Se bramate damar from Serse (HWV.40)

rejected.reason: a Menuet excerpt plus a separate anthem vs the whole Trio Sonata HWV 399; excerpt-vs-whole, not a merge; lengths 9m vs 12m
titles: Menuet, from 'Trio Sonata in G, HWV 399'; O Lord, whose Mercies numberless
Trio Sonata in G, HWV 399 - Sonata per orchestra, Op 5/4

rejected.reason: a 3-prelude airing from Preludes Book 1 (~7m) is a different, smaller selection than the two 5-prelude airings (~15m) it was fused with on shared tokens; these two pairs link the 3-prelude title to each 5-prelude title and are not merges
titles: Danseuses de Delphes; Le vent dans la plaine; Minstrels (from Preludes, Book 1)
Danseuses de Delphes, La cathédrale engloutie, La danse de Puck, Le vent dans la plaine, Minstrels - from Preludes (Book 1)
Danseuses de Delphes, La cathÃ©drale engloutie, La danse de Puck, Le vent dans la plaine, Minstrels - from Preludes (Book 1)

rejected.reason: two different movements of the 4 Romantic Pieces Op.75 — Allegro appassionato (No.3) and Allegro moderato (No.1); the distinguishing word is a bare tempo marking, so conflict() does not catch it and the lengths match (~3m each)
titles: Allegro appassionato (4 Romantic Pieces for violin and piano, Op 75)
Allegro moderato (4 Romantic Pieces for violin and piano, Op 75)

rejected.reason: two different pieces from one recital — a Follia and the Op.5 No.5 sonata — aired the same night (2019-12-26, 28 min apart), so cannot be one work re-aired; both reduce to 'G minor, Op 5' with the same performers
titles: Follia in G minor, Op 5
Violin Sonata in G minor, Op 5 no 5

rejected.reason: different church sonatas, different keys — Op.1 No.5 in B flat vs Op.1 No.1 in F. conflict() misses it: numbers pool to {1,5} vs {1} (Op 1 and No 1 both being 1 collapse in the set), and the subset rule then reads {1}<={1,5} as 'merely omits a number'; the keys are on different notes so no same-note mode clash fires either
titles: Sonate da chiesa in B flat major, Op.1 No.5
Sonate da chiesa in F major, Op 1 No 1

rejected.reason: three different liturgical items — the Christmas Epistle, Evangelium and Collecta — aired consecutively on 2022-12-25; fused on the shared 'Spangenberg-Graduale of 1545' boilerplate, but they are three parts of one liturgy, not one work re-aired
titles: Christmas Epistle: Sung reading according to the Spangenberg-Graduale of 1545
Christmas Evangelium: Sung reading according to the Spangenberg-Graduale of 1545
Collecta: Sung prayer according to the Spangenberg-Graduale of 1545

rejected.reason: two different dances from Orfeo ed Euridice — Dance of the Blessed Spirits and Dance of the Furies — fused on the shared 'ballet music ... Orphee et Euridice'
titles: Ballet music: 'Dance of the Blessed Spirits' - from 'Orphée et Euridice'
Dance of the Furies (ballet music from 'Orphee et Euridice')

rejected.reason: two different pieces from the Suite espanola Op.47 — Asturias and Sevilla — aired the same day in one recital, fused on 'from Spanish Suite op 47'
titles: Asturias op 232/1 from 'Spanish Suite op 47'
Sevilla - from Spanish Suite op 47

rejected.reason: two different sections of the Amphitryon music — Intermede IV (~12m) and the Divertissements (~28m) — fused on the long shared title stem; the length mismatch confirms distinct works
titles: Quatre Intermèdes et Divertissements for Molière's comedy 'Amphitryon' (Paris-Stockholm, 1785-87) - Intermède IV
Quatre Intermèdes et Divertissements for Molière's comedy 'Amphitryon' (VB.27) (Paris-Stockholm, 1785-87) - Divertissements

rejected.reason: two different movements of the Mass 'O admirabile commercium' — Agnus Dei and Credo — fused on the shared 'from Mass ...' stem
titles: Agnus Dei from Mass 'O admirabile commercium'
Credo from Mass 'O admirabile commercium'

rejected.reason: two different movement-pairs of the Mass a 5 — Kyrie and Gloria vs Sanctus and Benedictus — fused on the shared 'from Mass a 5'
titles: Kyrie and Gloria from Mass a 5
Sanctus and Benedictus from Mass a 5

rejected.reason: movements I and IV of the string quartet 'The Four Quarters' — Nightfalls and The Twenty-Fifth Hour; distinct movements fused on the shared 'from The Four Quarters' stem, no digit/key/part signal for conflict() to catch; lengths 7m vs 4m
titles: Nightfalls, from 'The Four Quarters'
Twenty-Fifth Hour, from 'The Four Quarters'

rejected.reason: Canzona 19 ('La Capriola') vs Canzona 17 ('La Diodata') from Il primo libro delle Canzoni; the numbers are spelled in Italian (decimanova/decimasettima) so conflict() sees no digit clash, and the long shared 'from Il primo libro delle Canzoni' stem fused them
titles: Canzona decimanova, detta 'La Capriola', Canto e Bass for cornet, sackbut, organ and chitarrone - from Il primo Libro delle Canzoni (Rome 1628)
Canzona decimasettima, detta 'La Diodata', à due bassi, for curtal, bass sackbut, organ and chitarrone - from Il primo libro delle Canzoni (Rome 1628)

rejected.reason: two distinct motets — Ad te levavi oculos meos and Nos autem gloriari oportet — fused via a third title that aired both together; the two single-motet airings are separate works, not one re-aired
titles: Ad te levavi oculos meos - motet for 4 voices
Nos autem gloriari oportet - motet for 4 voices
Nos autem gloriari oportet; Ad te levavi oculos meos - motet for 4 voices

rejected.reason: the whole improvised concert (~73m) vs a single section, Part IIc (~7m); excerpt vs whole, not a re-airing
titles: The Köln Concert
The Köln Concert - Part IIc

rejected.reason: the Chaconne and the Rondo are two distinct movements of the Pièces de Viole, Book 3; a combined 'Chaconne & Rondo' airing fused the two separate single-movement airings
titles: Chaconne & Rondo (from "Pièces de Viole, Book 3 no.4, Paris, 1711")
Chaconne, from "Pièces de Viole, Book 3, Paris, 1711
Rondo, from 'Pièces de Viole, Book 3, Paris, 1711

rejected.reason: a two-section excerpt of the Credo (Crucifixus and Resurrexit) vs the whole Missa Sancti Henrici (~37m); excerpt vs whole, fused on the long shared instrumentation string
titles: Crucifux and Resurrexit from the Credo from Missa Sancti Henrici, for 5 soloists, 5-part chorus, 5 trumpets, timpani, 2 violins, 3 violas, violone, and organ (1701)
Missa Sancti Henrici, for 5 soloists, 5-part chorus, 5 trumpets, timpani, 2 violins, 3 violas, violone, and organ

rejected.reason: two different suites from the 'Erster Fleiß' collection — one in C major for ensemble, one in D minor for gambas; different keys on different notes (so no same-note mode clash) and different scoring, fused on the shared collection name
titles: Suite for ensemble in C major from the collection "Erster Fleiss"
Suite in D minor for gambas - from the collection 'Erster Fleiß'
